changes_summary: Targeted iteration. (1) issue_hints-1, scope-1, all_locations, FLAG-301 — added a new dedicated Step 6 that names `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` at useTimelineCommit.ts:110-255 and commits to **deleting it entirely**, plus updates `materializeData()` to drop the reconciler call. Verified the function exists and matches the critique. (2) issue_hints-2 — committed to **dropping** the reverse auto-restore behavior at :172-246 (delete a video-mode group's video clip → group is left empty, undo is the recovery path). Documented as a deliberate product change with rationale that it's exactly the kind of hidden reverse coupling the rollback eliminates. (3) scope-2 — explicit delete-shot ↔ reconciler coupling: delete-shot now deletes clips and entry in one commit with no auto-restore, stated in Step 6.5 and Step 11.3. (4) correctness — Step 9.2 now includes the resolveOverlaps caveat: same-track grouped drag re-derives clipIds from post-resolve order and includes a `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in the same `rows` commit if the order changed. (5) Step 13 now explicitly renders empty groups as a degraded placeholder instead of filtering them out, which is the rendering side of dropping auto-restore. Renumbered to 16 steps. No scope growth — the new Step 6 is inside `useTimelineCommit.ts` which was already named.

success_criteria.criterion: Loading a config with legacy `pinnedShotGroups[].children` yields runtime groups in the new soft-tag shape, and saving that same timeline emits no `start` or `children` fields.

No runtime load/save/commit path re-derives clip timing or track placement from shot-group child geometry; `clips[]` remains the only source of truth for `at` and duration. This includes `rowsToConfig()`, `clonePinnedShotGroups()`, `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry`, and the deletion of `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()`.

`reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` is deleted from `useTimelineCommit.ts` and `materializeData()` no longer calls any in-commit projection step. Every commit's pinned-groups list passes through unchanged unless the caller mutated it via `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`.

Deleting a video-mode group's video clip leaves the group entry in place with empty `clipIds` and does not auto-insert image clips. The empty group renders as a degraded placeholder rather than disappearing. Undo is the documented recovery path.
`TimelineEditMutation`'s `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` field is typed as the soft-tag shape (no `start`/`children`) on both the `rows` and `config` variants.
Dragging the right edge of the last clip in a shot changes only that clip's duration and the group's rendered right boundary follows.
Dragging the left edge of the first clip in a shot changes only that clip's start/duration and the group's rendered left boundary follows.

Dragging an interior boundary inside a shot changes only the two adjacent clips while preserving the rest of the group, and the collapsed `clip` resize session carries both clip ids and clamps without regressing the previous paired-preview behavior.

Dragging a grouped clip body translates the full group together. Same-track grouped drag commits as `type: 'rows'`; if `resolveOverlaps` reorders member clips on the row, the same commit also carries a `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` (soft-tag) updating `clipIds`. New-track grouped drag commits as a single `type: 'config'` action whose `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` is the soft-tag shape and whose `nextResolvedConfig` contains the new track plus the moved clips — no split commits, no projection geometry.

Grouped cross-track moves initiated from `useTimelineTrackManagement` (drag, keyboard move-selected, create-track-and-move) all route through the soft-tag model: existing-track moves use `type: 'rows'`, new-track moves use `type: 'config'` with the soft-tag override. No calls to `moveGroup()` and no projection geometry remain.

`interactionStateRef` is created in `useTimelineState.ts` and shared with `useTimelineSave`, `useTimelinePersistence`, `usePollSync`, `useClipDrag`, and `useClipResize`; auto-save does not execute during an active drag or resize, and the newest pending save flushes after the interaction ends.

Poll-sync and conflict reloads do not overwrite local edits during an active drag or resize interaction; only the newest deferred remote payload is re-evaluated against current local state after the interaction ends.

`usePinnedShotGroups` auto-sync preserves live timing of surviving member clips: added images append at the group right edge, removed images delete only their member clip with no trailing shift, and a shot-image reorder with unchanged membership is a deliberate timeline no-op (documented in code).
Delete-shot deletes member clips via `rows` and removes the group entry in the same commit, with no auto-restore from `imageClipSnapshot`.
Shot label menu actions and per-clip context-menu lockdown still work under the soft-tag model.
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

The video-editor test suite passes after updating intentional expectations across migrate, serialize, timeline-save-utils, render-bounds.validation, useTimelineCommit, useTimelineTrackManagement, useExternalDrop, useClipEditing, useClipDrag, useShotGroups, usePollSync, and persistence tests. `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` is deleted. No new failures beyond the known unrelated pre-existing TimelineCanvas assertion if it still remains.
Net LOC across shot-group-related files decreases (deletion of `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows` alone is ~140 lines).
An unchanged timeline produces the same clip-level data for Remotion preview before and after the refactor.
Manual browser smoke testing confirms the two reported resize/save-reset symptoms no longer reproduce.

assumptions: The brief's rollback direction is correct: a structural removal of projection is simpler and safer than incremental fixes.
`(shotId, trackId)` remains the identity for a pinned group; no new stable group id is introduced.
Stored `clipIds` is ordered left-to-right by live clip `at` and is rebuilt whenever membership or position changes.
Track creation must remain a `type: 'config'` commit because `TimelineEditMutation.rows` (`useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65`) does not carry a `tracks` field.

`reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` is deleted outright rather than rewritten. Under the soft-tag model there is no work for an in-commit projection step to do; `clips[]` already reflects the caller's intent.

The reverse auto-restore behavior (deleting a video-mode group's only video clip silently reinserts image clips from `imageClipSnapshot`) is **dropped** as a deliberate product change. It is exactly the kind of hidden reverse coupling that produces the bug class this rollback eliminates, and undo is the standard recovery path. Empty groups render as a degraded placeholder, not invisible.

Same-track grouped drag emits `type: 'rows'`; if `resolveOverlaps` reorders member clips on the row the same commit also carries a soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` updating `clipIds`. The override is omitted when order is unchanged.
Auto-sync of pinned groups preserves live timing of surviving members; reorder-only image-list changes are an intentional timeline no-op.
`pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` is deleted outright; its role is covered by the new sequence test in Step 15 plus per-operation tests in Steps 7–10.
